title: Age and gender distortion in online media and large language models
methods: large-scale crowdsourced face annotation (Amazon Mechanical Turk, 6,392 coders)
ground-truth celebrity age benchmarking (IMDb-Wiki, CACD datasets)
geometry-of-culture word-embedding analysis (cosine distance, gender and age poles)
ChatGPT resume audit via prompt engineering (~39,560 resumes)
Pearson correlation, OLS regression with occupation and participant fixed effects
sensitivity analyses across 9 LLMs and multiple image datasets
data_sources: US Bureau of Labor Statistics (occupation age/gender/earnings)

data_scope.data_collection_period: Google Images: August 2020; ChatGPT audit: June 2024; experiment: November 2023
data_scope.geographic_scope: US-centered (Census benchmarks); Google Images collected from US servers (robustness: 6 global cities)

core_conclusion: Despite no systematic age differences between women and men in the US workforce per Census data, women are represented as younger than men across ~1.4 million images/videos from Google, Wikipedia, IMDb, Flickr and YouTube, and in nine language models. The age gap is starkest for high-status, high-earning occupations. Google Image search causally amplifies this bias in human hiring beliefs (~5.5-year age shift), and ChatGPT structurally encodes gendered ageism by generating younger/less-experienced resumes for women and rating older male applicants highest.
why_it_matters: First large-scale multimodal ground-truth test of stereotype accuracy using biological age as an objective anchor; causal evidence (pre-registered experiment) that algorithmic content shapes hiring-age beliefs; ChatGPT resume audit reveals structural gendered ageism at scale affecting 400M+ weekly users; cross-modal replication rules out modality-specific artifacts; provides generalizable methodology for auditing intersectional AI bias.
why_nature: Headline-setting scale (~1.4M images, 9 LLMs) and cross-modality novelty; objective ground-truth benchmarking resolves epistemological impasse in stereotype research; direct policy urgency (ChatGPT hiring bias, Google amplification); methodology is a generalizable audit platform, not just a one-time result.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatially disaggregated LLM bias audit -> digital-divide indicator layer for GeoAI health-geography analyses
Longitudinal geometry-of-culture pipeline -> audit for sociodemographic biases in geospatial foundation models I fine-tune
Census-benchmarking design generalized -> compare LLM neighbourhood descriptions to actual R2SFCA accessibility surfaces
Pre-registered image-experiment template -> test whether AI health imagery shifts patient care-seeking age norms
Resume quality score gradient + job-posting geocoding -> disentangle algorithmic and spatial dimensions of occupational gender inequality

related.title: Socio-demographic gaps in pain management guided by large language models
related.relation: LLM sociodemographic bias in clinical recommendations — parallel domain (health vs. hiring), same audit concern

related.title: On responsible machine learning datasets emphasizing fairness, privacy and regulatory norms with examples in biometrics and healthcare
related.relation: responsible ML dataset standards — directly applicable to the image/LLM training corpora audited in this paper

related.title: Algorithmic fairness and bias mitigation for clinical machine learning with deep reinforcement learning
related.relation: algorithmic fairness and bias mitigation methods — technical complement to the paper's bias-detection findings

related.title: City representation in Soviet propaganda and geographical biases in cultural data
related.relation: cultural representation biases in large-scale data — methodologically parallel (distortion of ground truth in media corpora)

related.title: Representation in science and trust in scientists in the USA
related.relation: demographic representation gaps and downstream trust/behavior — sociological parallel to age-gender distortion effects on hiring

figures.caption: Women represented as significantly younger than men across 10 image/video datasets (~1.3M images) using human coders, ground-truth ages and ML classifications
Age–gender association correlation (r=0.87) in GPT-2 Large across 3,495 WordNet social categories — replicated in 8 additional LLMs
Pre-registered Google Image search experiment (n=459): uploading a female occupation image shifts age estimates ~5.5 years younger vs. male image, amplifying hiring-age bias
ChatGPT resume audit: female names generate 1.6 fewer years of age and 0.92 fewer years of experience; resume quality scores rise with age, more so for men
